LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT RAMI BALAT MOTION TO DISMISS FOR FAILURE TO PROSECUTE. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION IS DENIED. INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE PRESENTED THAT PLAINTIFF WAS DILATORY IN PROSECUTING THIS ACTION. PLAINTIFF ACTIVELY RESPONDED TO THE OSC AND FILED A MOTION TO VACATE THE ARBITRATION ORDER, BOTH WITHIN THE PAST YEAR. PLAINTIFF ALSO SETTLED WITH THE OTHER DEFENDANT IN MID-2008. DEFENDANT FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE ANY REAL PREJUDICE. PUBLIC POLICY SUPPORTS HEARING THIS CASE ON THE MERITS. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Ntc Of Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute
Off Calendar Jun-18-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT RAMI BALAT Motion to Dismiss for Failure to Prosecute. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is denied. Insufficient evidence presented that plaintiff was dilatory in prosecuting this action. Plaintiff actively responded to the OSC and filed a motion to vacate the arbitration order, both within the past year. Plaintiff also settled with the other defendant in mid-2008. Defendant fails to demonstrate any real prejudice. Public policy supports hearing this case on the merits. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Re-Notice Of Motion To Quash Subpoenas
Off Calendar Apr-02-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant Maher Muhawieh's motion to quash subpoenas is granted. The Court finds that plaintiff misused the discovery process within the meaning of CCP Section 2023.010 (a). The request for monetary sanctions is denied as the declaration does not comply with CCP Section 2023.040. Prevailing party to submit form of order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.
